---
title: "epy_mdr — Quarto demo"
author: "Ing. Angel Navarro-Mora M.Sc."
date: "2026-06-09"
bibliography: sample.bib
---

# Introduction {#sec-intro}

A short technical note that exercises **Quarto** features inside
`epy_mdr`: YAML front matter, fenced-div callouts, code blocks
with syntax highlighting, Pandoc tables with captions, implicit
figures and LaTeX math.

> The same Pandoc render is used for the on-screen preview and the
> PDF export, so the printed page matches what you see.

# Callouts {#sec-callouts}

Quarto callouts are written as fenced divs and support an optional
`title` attribute.

::: {.callout-note}
A `note` callout. Useful for context that is not critical to the
reader's main task.
:::

::: {.callout-tip title="Pro tip"}
Use `Ctrl + P` to export this exact view to PDF. The print
stylesheet adapts margins, fonts and code blocks automatically.
:::

::: {.callout-warning}
Empirical formulas have a **range of validity**. Always check the
parameters against the code before applying them.
:::

::: {.callout-important title="Read this first"}
The minimum shear reinforcement in @sec-math is the *larger* of two
expressions, never the average and never rounded down.
:::

::: {.callout-caution title="Safety critical"}
A wrong shear-reinforcement detail can trigger a brittle failure
with **no warning**.
:::

# Images {#sec-images}

Two figures are embedded with relative paths, so they only resolve
when this file is opened from its real location on disk.

![Pushover capacity curve (base shear vs. roof drift).](../Books/modules/M14_sismica_control/figures/F4_2_curva_capacidad.png){#fig-capacity width=80%}

![The Eiffel Tower — an early lattice-tower benchmark for wind-dominated tall structures.](../Books/modules/M00_introduccion_ingenieria_estructural/figures/F3.1_eiffel_actual.png){#fig-eiffel width=70%}

# Tables {#sec-tables}

: Strength-design load combinations (ASCE 7). {#tbl-loads}

| #   | Combination                          | Governs                |
| --- | ------------------------------------ | ---------------------- |
| 1   | $1.4\,D$                             | Pure gravity           |
| 2   | $1.2\,D + 1.6\,L + 0.5\,(L_r,S,R)$   | Live load              |
| 3   | $1.2\,D + 1.6\,(L_r,S,R) + (L,0.5W)$ | Roof live / snow       |
| 4   | $1.2\,D + 1.0\,W + L + 0.5\,(L_r,S)$ | Wind                   |
| 5   | $0.9\,D + 1.0\,W$                    | Wind uplift / overturn |
| 6   | $1.2\,D + 1.0\,E + L + 0.2\,S$       | Seismic                |
| 7   | $0.9\,D + 1.0\,E$                    | Seismic overturning    |

: Typical material properties. {#tbl-materials}

| Material         | $f'_c$ / $f_y$ | $E$ (GPa) | $\nu$ | $\gamma$ (kN/m³) |
| ---------------- | -------------- | --------- | ----- | ---------------- |
| Concrete C25     | 25 MPa         | 25.0      | 0.20  | 24.0             |
| Concrete C35     | 35 MPa         | 28.5      | 0.20  | 24.0             |
| Rebar A615 Gr.60 | 420 MPa        | 200.0     | 0.30  | 77.0             |
| Steel A36        | 250 MPa        | 200.0     | 0.30  | 77.0             |
| Steel A992       | 345 MPa        | 200.0     | 0.30  | 77.0             |

# Equations {#sec-math}

Inline math: $\lambda = k L / r$ with $r = \sqrt{I/A}$.

ACI 318-19 §9.6.3.4 — minimum shear reinforcement:

$$
A_{v,\min} \;=\; \max\!\left(
    0.062\,\sqrt{f'_c}\,\frac{b_w\,s}{f_{yt}},\;
    0.35\,\frac{b_w\,s}{f_{yt}}
\right)
$$ {#eq-avmin}

Simply supported beam, uniform load $w$, length $L$:

$$
\delta_{\max} \;=\; \frac{5\,w\,L^{4}}{384\,E\,I}
\qquad\text{at } x = L/2
$$ {#eq-delta}

SDOF equation of motion under ground acceleration $\ddot u_g$:

$$
m\,\ddot u(t) + c\,\dot u(t) + k\,u(t)
\;=\; -\,m\,\ddot u_g(t)
$$ {#eq-eom}

# Code {#sec-code}

Standard fenced code block with a language tag (highlighted by
Pandoc's skylighting engine):

```python
def av_min(fc: float, fyt: float, bw: float, s: float) -> float:
    """Return ACI 318-19 minimum shear reinforcement area (mm^2)."""
    a = 0.062 * fc**0.5 * bw * s / fyt
    b = 0.35 * bw * s / fyt
    return max(a, b)
```

Quarto also accepts the engine-style fence; here it is rendered but
not executed:

````qmd
```{python}
import numpy as np
np.linspace(0, 1, 5)
```
````

# Lists and quotes {#sec-misc}

- [x] YAML front matter as document header
- [x] Quarto callouts with optional titles
- [x] Figures with captions and IDs
- [x] Tables with captions and IDs
- [x] Display math with `{#eq-...}` labels
- [ ] Cross-reference resolution (`@fig-…`, `@eq-…`) — future work

> *"All models are wrong, but some are useful."* — G. E. P. Box

# References {.unnumbered}

This document cites @box1987empirical for the famous remark above,
@aci318min for the shear reinforcement formula in @sec-math, and
@tacoma1941collapse for the wind-induced collapse mentioned next to
the Eiffel image in @sec-images.
